claim: Thigh-length graduated compression stockings (GCS) reduced deep vein thrombosis in patients admitted to hospital who are immobile because of acute stroke.

result.claim: Thigh-length graduated compression stockings (GCS) reduced deep vein thrombosis in patients admitted to hospital who are immobile because of acute stroke.
result.reasoning: The claim is refuted by evidence from a multicentre, randomised controlled trial (CLOTS trial 1, Paper ID: 11718220). This study, which directly assessed the effectiveness of thigh-length graduated compression stockings (GCS) in patients admitted to hospital with acute stroke, found no significant reduction in deep vein thrombosis (DVT). Specifically, the primary outcome (occurrence of DVT) was 10.0% in the GCS group and 10.5% in the control group, resulting in a non-significant absolute risk reduction of 0.5% (95% CI -1.9% to 2.9%). The study concluded that these data "do not lend support to the use of thigh-length GCS in patients admitted to hospital with acute stroke." Furthermore, the study reported that adverse events such as skin breaks, ulcers, blisters, and skin necrosis were significantly more common in patients using GCS.

evidence.text: Total, vertebral, nonvertebral, and hip fractures were significantly lower in refill-compliant and persistent patients.
evidence.source: Adherence to Bisphosphonate Therapy and Fracture Rates in Osteoporotic Women: Relationship to Vertebral and Nonvertebral Fractures From 2 US Claims Databases

evidence.text: The primary outcome occurred in 10.0% of patients allocated to thigh-length GCS.
evidence.source: Effectiveness of thigh-length graduated compression stockings to reduce the risk of deep vein thrombosis after stroke (CLOTS trial 1): a multicentre, randomised controlled trial

evidence.text: Patients were admitted to hospital within 1 week of an acute stroke.
evidence.source: Effectiveness of thigh-length graduated compression stockings to reduce the risk of deep vein thrombosis after stroke (CLOTS trial 1): a multicentre, randomised controlled trial

evidence.text: A technician undertook compression Doppler ultrasound of both legs at about 7-10 days and, when practical, again at 25-30 days after enrolment.
evidence.source: Effectiveness of thigh-length graduated compression stockings to reduce the risk of deep vein thrombosis after stroke (CLOTS trial 1): a multicentre, randomised controlled trial

evidence.text: The mean (SD) reduction in right middle cerebral artery area on day 7 after subarachnoid hemorrhage was 8% [9%] in nitrite-treated monkeys.
evidence.source: Nitrite infusions to prevent delayed cerebral vasospasm in a primate model of subarachnoid hemorrhage.

evidence.text: Patients were randomly allocated to routine care plus thigh-length GCS (n=1256) or routine care plus avoidance of GCS (n=1262).
evidence.source: Effectiveness of thigh-length graduated compression stockings to reduce the risk of deep vein thrombosis after stroke (CLOTS trial 1): a multicentre, randomised controlled trial
